column1: # dbip-city-lite.fixture.csv -- TINY committed test fixture (quick task 260630-hct).
# Format mirrors the compact range table that ip-geo.js parses and that
# scripts/refresh-dbip-dataset.mjs emits from the upstream DB-IP CSV:
# where start/end are inclusive uint32 forms of the IPv4 range bounds. Rows must
# be sorted ascending by start_ip_int (the module binary-searches on that key).
# Lines beginning with '#' and blank lines are ignored by the parser.
# This is NOT real geolocation data -- it is a handful of deterministic ranges
# (well-known/reserved IPv4 blocks) chosen only so the geo + aggregation tests
# run offline. The real ~100MB DB-IP IP-to-City Lite artifact is generated by
# the refresh script and is NOT committed (see data/README.md + .gitignore).
# Attribution for the real dataset: IP Geolocation by DB-IP (https://db-ip.com)
